notes: Calibration data points — UI % vs tracked token counts. Used to derive actual Anthropic metering formula.

data_points.label: DP1 — initial calibration (pre-session-summary)
data_points.source: conversation summary — exact token counts not directly observed

tracker_tokens.note: back-calculated from implied 8.935M limit — not directly observed

cache_tokens.sonnet_cache_write: unknown — not tracked at time of DP1

implied_limits_output_only.caveat: if cache writes are metered this will be wrong

data_points.label: DP2 — end of billing week (31h before reset)

tracker_tokens.note: from raw JSONL scan, ~6 days window (may include a few pre-Tuesday files)

implied_limits_output_only.caveat: wildly inconsistent with DP1 — output-only metering does not explain the numbers

implied_limits_output_plus_cache_write_2.comment: if cache_write is metered at 0.25x output rate — still speculative

data_points.label: DP3 — end of billing week (~6.5h before reset)

discrepancies_flagged.sonnet_pct_tool_vs_ui: Tool: 48.5% vs UI: 92% — 1.9x gap. Calibrated limit of 5,023,178 appears ~2x too high.
discrepancies_flagged.reset_time_tool_vs_ui: Tool says reset in 2h (midnight UTC). UI says reset in 6h33m (~05:00 UTC). Tool is using wrong reset anchor — Anthropic likely resets at midnight US Central (CDT = UTC-5 = 05:00 UTC), not midnight UTC.
discrepancies_flagged.implied_sonnet_limit: 2,434,131 output tokens / 0.92 = ~2,645,000 — suggests true output limit is ~2.6M, not 5.0M
discrepancies_flagged.implied_all_models_limit: 8,697,584 output tokens / 0.77 = ~11,295,000 — closer to current calibrated 13M but still off

data_points.action_needed: Two bugs to fix in usage-check.py: (1) reset anchor should be Tuesday 05:00 UTC not 00:00 UTC; (2) re-calibrate immediately after Tuesday reset with fresh reading at near-0% usage to get clean limit derivation.

analysis.key_finding: Cache writes are 11.4M Sonnet tokens vs 2.6M Sonnet output tokens — a 4.4:1 ratio. If cache writes are metered at even 25% rate, the weighted usage nearly doubles. This is the most likely explanation for the DP1 vs DP2 inconsistency.
analysis.why_dp1_dp2_are_inconsistent: DP1 output ~2.0M → 22.3%. DP2 output 2.63M → 42%. Output only went up 630K but UI jumped 20 percentage points. Cache writes accumulated massively across the week (orchestrator reads same large context files each session).
analysis.cache_read_note: 510M cache read tokens — almost certainly NOT metered or metered at negligible rate. These are the orchestrator re-reading the same context (PROJECTS.md, WORKLOG.md, etc.) with cache hits.
analysis.fresh_input_note: Only 110K fresh input tokens. Almost all 'input' is served from cache. This is expected for the orchestrator pattern.
analysis.next_data_point_needed: A reading early in the next billing week (Tuesday AM, before much work is done) with BOTH UI% and our token counts including cache_writes would let us solve for the cache_write metering rate.

current_limits_in_projects_md.basis: DP2 output-only back-calculation — conservative but likely incorrect formula
